mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals impending corporate revenue contraction, prompting equity investors to price in lower earnings growth and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and industrial production indicates a structural shift in economic momentum where residential investment offsets manufacturing weakness, creating a net positive demand impulse that lifts equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain more than 2% of forward return variance in out-of-sample testing, or the signal flips direction during recessions.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity multiples, causing the S&P 500 to underperform over the next quarter as valuation expansion stalls.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5% for consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth reflects broad-based economic expansion that directly boosts corporate profit margins, producing a persistent positive trend in S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between quarterly GDP growth surprises and weekly SPX returns falls below 0.10 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below the 110 index level signals supply chain bottlenecks or demand destruction that directly reduces corporate throughput, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO drops below 110, or the signal produces false breakdowns during normal cyclical fluctuations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which historically precedes sustained equity bull markets and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 100k in 3 months AND UNRATE falls
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over any 3-year rolling period, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in residential construction activity combined with rising mortgage debt outstanding indicates strong household balance sheet expansion, which fuels consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined housing and mortgage debt signal predicts negative returns during the 2006-2007 period, or the forward return differential drops below 0.5% over a 2-year test window.
